summary.what: Rename ledger files to drop colons so 'go install' works on tagged versions
summary.why: Colons in .timbers/*.json break Go's module zip format and proxy, blocking 'go install ...@latest' for all v0.16.x and v0.17.x; user-reported install failure
summary.how: Added IDToFilename/FilenameToID helpers (only flip HH:MM:SS separators), made entryPath emit canonical dashed names, added legacyEntryPath fallback for reads, transparent on-write cleanup of legacy sibling, bulk migration via FileStorage.MigrateLegacyFilenames + 'timbers doctor --fix', and ran the migration on this repo's 146 existing entries

notes: Considered a hard cutover (delete legacy support entirely once we tag) vs indefinite backward-compat reads; chose indefinite reads because forks and downstream consumers may have entries written by older binaries — they should not need to upgrade in lockstep. The colon files in pre-v0.18 git history remain readable by post-v0.18 binaries forever. Also considered mangling the ID itself (drop colons everywhere) but rejected — the canonical ISO 8601 timestamp is meaningful and used in tons of places (CLI args, JSON content); only the filesystem encoding needed to change.
